text:
In software development, testing plays a vital role in ensuring code quality. There are different levels of testing. First, unit tests check small pieces of code, like functions or methods, to see if they work correctly alone. Then, integration tests verify that different parts of the application work together properly, such as a database connecting with a web server.

Many developers use test-driven development, or TDD. In this approach, they write tests before writing the actual code. This forces them to think carefully about the desired outcome. Overall, a strong set of tests gives developers confidence. When code changes are needed, they can run the tests and know immediately if anything has broken. This makes updates safer and more efficient.
